prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_svr', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.36) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. FT_catboost dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA excels at seasonal tracking, with early/late horizon specialists differing substantially. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and temporal horizons.

You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_catboost", "THETA", "CWT_rf", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ETS", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_svr", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3602,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.36) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. FT_catboost dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA excels at seasonal tracking, with early/late horizon specialists differing substantially. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and temporal horizons."
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["best_per_horizon_by_validation", "dba_combination", "inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.10"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has only baselines (baseline_mean, robust_median, trimmed_mean variants). Must add weighted/selection candidates per rules. relative_spread_mean=0.2997≥0.25 → DBA favored for disagreement handling. n_unique_winners=7≥3 → best_per_horizon_by_validation favored. inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.10 shows POCID=66.67 in universe leaderboard (tied with robust_median). n_windows=3≤4 → recommended shrinkage=0.35, top_k=5 from knobs.", "changes": ["added best_per_horizon_by_validation for high unique winners", "added dba_combination for high disagreement", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.10 for POCID performance"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.36) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. FT_catboost dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA excels at seasonal tracking, with early/late horizon specialists differing substantially. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and temporal horizons.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only baselines (baseline_mean, robust_median, trimmed_mean variants). Must add weighted/selection candidates per rules. relative_spread_mean=0.2997≥0.25 → DBA favored for disagreement handling. n_unique_winners=7≥3 → best_per_horizon_by_validation favored. inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.10 shows POCID=66.67 in universe leaderboard (tied with robust_median). n_windows=3≤4 → recommended shrinkage=0.35, top_k=5 from knobs.
changes: added best_per_horizon_by_validation for high unique winners
